title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2023-10-20
text: Patient is a 50-year-old female presenting with chest pain radiating to the left arm. History of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Current medications include atorvastatin 40mg and metoprolol 50mg. ECG shows ST elevation. Plan is to admit to CCU and schedule for urgent cardiac catheterization. Vitals: Temp 98.7F, HR 81, BP 158/94, RR 14, SpO2 98% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. Neck: Supple, no JVD or lymphadenopathy. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 and S2, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi, except as noted in HPI. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds active in all 4 quadrants. Extremities: No clubbing or cyanosis. Pulses 2+ bilaterally. Skin: Warm and dry, no rashes or lesions. Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor and sensory exams unremarkable. Assessment and Plan: As detailed above. Will follow up in 2 weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi, except as noted in HPI. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds active in all 4 quadrants. Extremities: No clubbing or cyanosis. Pulses 2+ bilaterally. Skin: Warm and dry, no rashes or lesions. Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor and sensory exams unremarkable. Assessment and Plan: As detailed above. Will follow up in 3 weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.
